title: Flaky Australian Grape Leaves Casserole
description: A malty, gooey, crowd-pleaser Australian dish featuring grape leaves, this casserole is a shojin-friendly option for any occasion. It combines the natural sweetness of grapes with the earthy flavor of leaves.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the rolled oats, malt syrup, nutritional yeast, salt, and black pepper.
In a separate pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the pan and cook for an additional minute.
In a large casserole dish, create a layer of grape leaves.
Spread half of the oat mixture over the grape leaves.
Repeat the layers, starting with the grape leaves, then the remaining oat mixture.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
